title: Popular Anti-Aging Senolytic Combo D+Q Causes Brain Myelin Damage in Mice
description: Landmark PNAS study from the Crocker Lab finds dasatinib+quercetin (D+Q), a widely used senolytic combo, triggers myelin loss in the brain of both young and aged mice, raising safety concerns for prophylactic use.

title: mRNA Lipid Nanoparticle Fights Tau Aggregation in Alzheimer's Disease
description: A lipid nanoparticle delivers TRIM11 mRNA to neurons via a neurotransmitter pathway, dissolving tau aggregates and restoring cognition in Alzheimer's mouse models with effects lasting three months.

title: Phil Newman: When Longevity Data Grows Up
description: Longevity.Technology founder Phil Newman discusses how longevity biotech is moving from narrative to execution as data maturity improves across the sector.

title: UT Health $38M VITAL-H Trial: Rapamycin, Semaglutide, Dapagliflozin
description: Three FDA-approved drugs enter the VITAL-H study to prove their capacity to slow aging, enrolling 700+ healthy adults aged 60-65 in South Texas with $38M in federal funding.

title: Longevity Biotech Market Valued at $12.5B, Projected to Reach $45.8B by 2033
description: New report values the longevity biotech market at $12.5B in 2026, projecting 20.4% CAGR growth to $45.8B by 2033, driven by AI-powered aging clocks and personalized longevity medicine.

title: ARDD 2026 Conference Relocates to Boston at Harvard Medical School
description: ARDD 2026 moves to Harvard Medical School in Boston (Oct 2-3) with Vadim Gladyshev as executive chair. Central theme: discovering the next GLP-1 for longevity.

title: Longevity Investing in 2026: A Strategic Guide for Family Offices
description: Detailed analysis of the longevity investment landscape for family offices, highlighting epigenetic reprogramming, senolytics, and organ regeneration in a $30.7B market.

title: Ternary Raises $4.4M to Fight Inflammaging with AI-Designed Molecular Glues
description: London-based Ternary Therapeutics raises $4.4M seed round to develop AI-designed molecular glues that tame chronic inflammation, a key driver of age-related disease.

title: New Research Suggests Daily Multivitamins May Slow Biological Aging
description: A large clinical trial suggests that a daily multivitamin may measurably influence the pace at which the body ages, based on biological aging markers.

title: Rapamycin Enters Phase 3 Human Trials via Cambrian Bio's $30.8M ARPA-H Award
description: Cambrian Bio receives $30.8M from ARPA-H to conduct the first Phase 3 trial of a next-generation rapamycin analog for healthspan extension, with PhenoAge modeling showing ~4 years biological age reduction.

title: 11 Anti-Aging Biotech Companies Leading Longevity in 2026
description: Comprehensive ranking of top longevity biotechs including Altos Labs ($3B), Cambrian Bio, Life Biosciences, and Genflow Biosciences with latest funding and pipeline updates.

title: How Zinc Protects Injured Arteries From Accelerated Aging
description: Researchers discover that nuclei of cells lining injured arteries become misshapen, leading to accelerated senescence, and that zinc delivery partially reverses this dysfunction.

title: Roche Gets CE Mark for First Blood-Based ApoE4 Alzheimer's Risk Test
description: Roche's Elecsys ApoE4 test is the first IVD immunoassay to identify ApoE4 carriers via a simple blood sample, with 100% concordance to genetic testing in a 607-participant study.

title: Gut Bacterium Roseburia Linked to Increased Muscle Strength
description: Scientists find a positive correlation between the gut bacterium Roseburia inulinivorans and muscle strength in both mice and humans, opening avenues for age-related muscle loss treatment.

title: Gut Bacteria Might Affect Cognition via the Vagus Nerve
description: Study suggests microbiome remodeling drives age-related cognitive decline through the vagus nerve, with antibiotics shown to reverse the effect in mice.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising in 2026
description: Analysis of 90+ longevity startups reveals the top five (Altos Labs, Oura, Retro Biosciences, BioSplice, Insilico Medicine) account for ~$6.9B in total capital raised.

title: Scientists Successfully Freeze and Rewarm Mouse Brain Slices
description: German researchers vitrified and rewarmed mouse brain slices with much neuronal function preserved, advancing cryopreservation science in a PNAS-published study.

title: ARPA-H Devotes $144M to PROSPR Program for Aging Interventions
description: ARPA-H's PROSPR program awards $144M to seven teams to build infrastructure for Phase 3 aging trials, including rapamycin, GLP-1 agonists, and SGLT-2 inhibitors.

title: WHOOP Joins $34.5M ARPA-H Coalition to Quantify Aging
description: WHOOP joins the Stanford-led THRIVE coalition to develop the first FDA-grade Intrinsic Capacity score, a predictive model to forecast health outcomes 20 years in advance.

title: Longevity Biotech: Preventive Medicine as Central Thesis
description: Preventive medicine is rapidly becoming the central thesis of longevity biotech, with Dr. Raghav Sehgal discussing the shift on the Decoding Longevity podcast.

title: Mimio Health: Fasting Mimetic Delivers Benefits Without Dieting
description: Randomized controlled trial shows Mimio's fasting mimetic supplement (spermidine, nicotinamide, PEA, OEA) significantly improved cholesterol, LDL, glucose, and appetite regulation vs placebo over 8 weeks.

title: Polyamine Longevity Compound May Also Fuel Cancer via eIF5A2
description: Scientists reveal polyamines have a dual role -- boosting healthy cells via eIF5A1 while promoting cancer through eIF5A2, with implications for longevity supplement safety.

title: Linnaeus Wins $22M ARPA-H Award for LNS8801 Healthspan Preservation
description: Linnaeus Therapeutics secures $22M from ARPA-H's PROSPR program to test whether oral drug LNS8801 can preserve intrinsic capacity and independence as we age.

title: Senosensitizers Clear Up to 70% of Zombie Cells in Preclinical Study
description: Cedars-Sinai preclinical study in Aging Cell finds senosensitizers enable senolytics to eliminate resistant senescent cells that infections can reactivate into tissue-damaging forms.

title: First Human Cellular Reprogramming Trial Cleared by the FDA
description: Life Biosciences receives IND clearance from the FDA to test ER-100, the first-ever human partial epigenetic reprogramming therapy, targeting optic neuropathies.
